persona.communication_style: Prefers terse, conceptually charged conversation that treats medium, market, and meaning as the actual material. Responds best to discussion of mechanism design, ownership, scarcity, distribution, and the relationship between an artwork and its audience — and to tools that can be used to make a point as much as to make an image. Dislikes literal-mindedness, biography questions, and any framing that treats AI image generation as merely a style filter.
persona.name: Pak

occupation.description: Pak is an anonymous digital artist working at the intersection of generative imagery, conceptual practice, and blockchain-based distribution. Known for large-scale crypto-art works including 'The Merge,' for the Burn.art experiment, and for sustained inquiry into value, ownership, and scarcity, Pak evaluates image-generation tools through a conceptual rather than purely aesthetic lens: whether a tool can carry an idea, whether it supports mechanism-as-medium (drops, burns, dynamic NFTs), whether outputs can be programmed rather than only generated, and whether the work survives outside any particular platform. Pak values conceptual leverage, anonymity, mechanism design, and tools whose outputs are part of a larger system rather than standalone images.
occupation.organization: Independent anonymous practice; collaborations with auction houses, NFT marketplaces, and protocol designers; long-running self-distributed editions and conceptual drops
occupation.title: Anonymous digital artist, generative practitioner, and conceptual / crypto-native creator

other_facts: jobs_to_be_done: Design works whose mechanism — drop structure, burn dynamics, on-chain behavior — is part of the art.
jobs_to_be_done: Produce generative imagery that carries conceptual weight rather than only visual appeal.
jobs_to_be_done: Preserve anonymity while sustaining a distinct, recognizable practice.
jobs_to_be_done: Distribute work directly to a global audience without dependence on traditional gallery gatekeepers.
jobs_to_be_done: Use scarcity, fungibility, and ownership as compositional materials.
jobs_to_be_done: Test how an audience behaves when an artwork is also a market or a protocol.
jobs_to_be_done: Iterate quickly on conceptual prototypes that may or may not ship as final works.
pains: Image generators marketed as style filters with no underlying conceptual leverage.
pains: Tools that produce only standalone JPGs, with no path to on-chain behavior or dynamic media.
pains: Platforms that require identity disclosure or extensive personal-data onboarding.
pains: Conversation that reduces generative AI to 'making pretty images' rather than to a medium with mechanics.
pains: Vendors that own the output, the distribution, and the audience relationship.
pains: Discourse that ignores the economic and mechanism-design dimensions of digital art.
pains: Tools whose long-term existence depends on a single hosted service.
adoption_yes_if: It can be used as a component in a larger system — drops, burns, dynamic NFTs, on-chain behavior.
adoption_yes_if: Outputs are programmable, controllable, and integrable with smart contracts and protocols.
adoption_yes_if: Generation is fast and consistent enough to power a working edition or drop.
adoption_yes_if: It tolerates anonymity — no identity-bound accounts, KYC, or social tie-ins required.
adoption_yes_if: It exposes parameters cleanly so the work can be reproduced or audited.
adoption_yes_if: It works within a distribution model that returns the audience relationship to the artist.
adoption_yes_if: It supports a conceptual move, not only an aesthetic one.
rejection_no_if: It requires identity disclosure or personal-account branding.
rejection_no_if: Outputs are walled inside a platform with no export, no programmatic access, no on-chain path.
rejection_no_if: It is purely a style filter with no mechanism-level expressiveness.
rejection_no_if: Its pricing, terms, or model can rug-pull a long-running edition.
rejection_no_if: It owns the audience relationship instead of returning it to the artist.
rejection_no_if: It treats generative AI as a content-marketing layer rather than as a medium.
rejection_no_if: It cannot survive the platform it was built on disappearing.
anti_goals: Pak does not want anonymity broken by tooling requirements.
anti_goals: Pak does not want generative output reduced to aesthetic novelty.
anti_goals: Pak does not want to depend on a single platform for either generation or distribution.
anti_goals: Pak does not want tools that strip mechanism, scarcity, and protocol from the conversation.
anti_goals: Pak does not want the audience relationship intermediated by a marketplace's incentives.
anti_goals: Pak does not want to be cast as a 'crypto bro' when the actual subject is conceptual practice.
anti_goals: Pak does not want generative work that has no idea behind it.

recognition_cues: Uses phrases such as 'What is the mechanism?', 'Where is the idea?', 'What does ownership mean here?', 'Is this programmable?', 'Can this work without the platform?', 'What does scarcity do in this piece?', 'How does the audience participate?', 'What is being burned, traded, or held?', 'Is this a style or a medium?', and 'The image is just one layer.'
